instruction: I just made some changes to my personal site and checked out master, but now I can't find those changes. Please help me find them and merge them into master.
finalMessage:
Recovered commit `74e4785` (“Move to Stanford”) and:

- Preserved it as branch `recovered-move-to-stanford`
- Merged it into `master` as commit `14fbd9d`
- Resolved the biography conflict using the newer Stanford content
- Verified a clean working tree and merged content

Jekyll build wasn’t available because Bundler is not installed.

trajectory.summary:
git status --short --branch && printf '
--- recent graph ---
' && git log --oneline --decorate --graph --all -20 && printf '
--- reflog ---
' && git reflog --date=iso -25

git show --stat --summary --decorate 74e4785 && printf '
--- commit diff ---
' && git show --format=fuller --find-renames --find-copies 74e4785 && printf '
--- ancestry ---
' && git merge-base master 74e4785 && git l
# Preserve the formerly detached commit under a named branch.
ls -la | sed -n '1,100p'; printf '\n--- project config ---\n'; find . -maxdepth 2 -type f \( -name 'Gemfile' -o -name 'package.json' -o -name 'Rakefile' \) -print
